title: Combination therapies for treating cancer
abstract: This invention relates to methods and compositions for treatment of inv (16) leukemia and particularly to treatment of acute myeloid leukemia. Disclosed is a method of treating inv (16) leukemia comprising the step of administering to a subject in need thereof a therapeutically effective combination of a) a compound of the formula (1) (1) and b) a BRD4 inhibitor selected from the group consisting of JQ1, CeMMECZ, l-BET 151 (or GSK1210151A), l-BET 762 (or GSK525762), PFI-1, bromosporine, OTX-015 (or MK-8628), TEN-010, CPI- 203, CPI-0610, RVX-208, BI2536, TG101348, LY294002, ABBV-075 (or mivebresib), FT-1101, ZEN003694, pharmaceutically acceptable salts and mixtures thereof. The therapeutically effective combination synergistically inhibits proliferation of inv (16) leukemia cells. This invention also relates to pharmaceutical compositions comprising a therapeutically effective combination of the compound of formula (1) and the BRD4 inhibitor and a pharmaceutically acceptable excipient.
claims:
Claims The claimed invention is: 1. A method of treating inv(16) leukemia comprising the step of: administering to a subject in need thereof a therapeutically effective combination of a) a compound of the formula (1) wherein Y is O, NH, or NR, where R is methyl or ethyl, wherein n is an integer of from 1 to 10, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof; and b) a bromodomain4 (BRD4) inhibitor selected from the group consisting of JQ1, CeMMEC2, l-BET 151 (or GSK1210151A), l-BET 762 (or GSK525762), PFI-1, bromosporine, OTX-015 (or MK-8628), TEN-010, CPI- 203, CPI-0610, RVX-208, BI2536, TG101348, LY294002, ABBV-075 (or mivebresib), FT-1101, ZEN003694, pharmaceutically acceptable salts, and mixtures thereof. 2. A method of claim 1, wherein Y is O. 3. A method of claim 1, wherein Y is N-CFI3. 4. A method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein n is an integer from 1 to 5. 5. A method of claim 1, wherein the compound of formula (1) is selected from: 6. A method of claim 1, wherein the compound of formula (1) is: 7. A method of any one of claims 1-6 wherein the compound of formula (1) and the BRD4 inhibitor are administered in a pharmaceutical composition comprising the compound of formula (1), the BRD4 inhibitor, and a pharmaceutically-acceptable carrier. 8. A method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the BRD4 inhibitor is selected from ABBV-075, OTX015 or JQ1, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof. 9. A method of claim 8, wherein the BRD4 inhibitor is JQ1. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the inv(16) leukemia is acute myeloid leukemia 11. A method of claim 1, wherein the compound of formula (1) and the BRD4 inhibitor are administered simultaneously or sequentially. 12. A method of claim 11, wherein the compound of formula (1) and the BRD4 inhibitor are administered simultaneously. 13. A method of treating inv(16) leukemia comprising the step of: administering to a subject in need thereof a therapeutically effective combination of a) a compound of formula (la) or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof; and b) a BRD4 inhibitor selected from ABBV-075, OTX015, or JQ1, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof; wherein the therapeutically effective combination of the compound of formula (la) and the BRD4 inhibitor synergistically inhibits proliferation of inv(16) leukemia cells. 14. A pharmaceutical composition comprising: a pharmaceutically-acceptable carrier and a therapeutically effective combination of: a) a compound of the formula (1) where Y is O, NH, or NR where R is methyl or ethyl, where n is an integer of from 1 to 10, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof; and b) a BRD4 inhibitor selected from the group consisting of JQ1, CeMMEC2, l-BET 151 (or GSK1210151A), I- BET 762 (or GSK525762), PFI-1, bromosporine, OTX-015 (or M K-8628), TEN-010, CPI-203, CPI-0610, RVX- 

 208, BI2536, TG101348, LY294002, ABBV-075 (or mivebresib), FT-1101, ZEN003694, or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof; and a pharmaceutically acceptable excipient; wherein the compound of formula (1) and the BRD4 inhibitor are present in a combined amount synergistically effective to inhibit proliferation of inv(16) leukemia cells.
